do_not_continue: draw-order H2 (earliness score / holdout inclusion frequency)
any re-parameterization of the exit-rank-heterogeneity H1 hypothesis

repo_only_limitation.description: This scouting round is REPO-ONLY. No external web search, no statistical tests, no data fetching, no DB writes. All findings are based solely on existing artifacts, roadmap, outputs/research/, analysis/, and lottery_api/data/ in the canonical repo.
repo_only_limitation.scope: Candidate matrix v0 only. Intended to feed P269B pre-registration design. No hit-rate improvement claim is made.
repo_only_limitation.model_budget_note: P269A originally designed for Opus/Fable; downgraded to Sonnet repo-only scouting due to model quota constraint.

draws_table_schema.jackpot_amount_populated: 0 rows for all lotteries (schema exists, no data)

draw_schedule_observed.BIG_LOTTO: Tue/Fri primary (raw table; canonical 2114 draws confirmed)

api_fields_confirmed_unavailable: machine/ball-set ID (P268C confirmed: TLCAPIWeB API exposes no such field)
api_fields_confirmed_available: lotteryDate (draw date — already in DB as 'date')
winnerCount / prizeLevel (TLCAPIWeB, not fetched, classified EV-only by P268A)

key_prior_findings.L82: DAILY_539 H001-H008 all REJECT/FAST_REJECT — signal space exhausted
key_prior_findings.L90_L91: BIG_LOTTO 49C6 indistinguishable from fair random (6 randomness tests)
key_prior_findings.L101_L102: Anti-crowd / sell-amount conditioning: unconditional edge remains negative; advisory-only
key_prior_findings.P245A_P245B: P245A ranked drawNumberAppear as Top-1 hit-rate; all others EV-only or diagnostic; P245B bias gate design complete
key_prior_findings.P213_H_REGIME_SEGMENTATION: Design-only, not authorized, P221F gate not passed
key_prior_findings.P253H_draw_history_feature: days_since_last_draw / calendar_gap / draw_index: NOT tested in P219 M10 sweep

candidate_matrix.reason: H1 PRIMARY_FAIL (DAILY_539 p=0.3051 >= alpha=0.01). Arc closed as DIAGNOSTICS_ONLY_NULL_CLOSURE. H2/H3 not authorized. P268D1 JSONL artifact remains available for a structurally different hypothesis (NOT H1/H2/H3 as designed), but that requires fresh pre-registration — not this scouting round.

candidate_matrix.signal_family: prize-tier / winner-count history as popularity proxy
candidate_matrix.source_hint_from_repo: P268A Track B classification: 'winnerCount / prize-distribution classified payout/EV-only'; active_task.md P268A-S entry; analysis/p268b_official_draw_order_positional_bias_audit.py
candidate_matrix.reason: Explicitly classified as Track B (payout/EV-only) by P268A-S. Winner count per prize tier affects expected payout share given a win, not P(win). L101/L102 already established that anti-crowd conditioning is advisory-only with unconditional edge remaining negative. Not a hit-rate signal.

candidate_matrix.signal_family: DAILY_539 ticket sales volume (sell_amount / total_amount) as regime signal
candidate_matrix.source_hint_from_repo: lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db draws.sell_amount and draws.total_amount — DAILY_539 has 5,861/5,885 rows populated covering full history 2007–2026; lottery_api/database.py:485-511 confirms ingestion path
candidate_matrix.reason: DAILY_539 is a fixed-prize lottery (not pari-mutuel), so ticket sales volume does not affect prize amounts. sell_amount is only useful as a crowd/popularity proxy (anti-crowd EV angle, L101/L102 already marginal). Does not plausibly affect P(win). BIG_LOTTO/POWER_LOTTO have 0 rows for jackpot/sell/total columns despite schema existing.
candidate_matrix.data_richness_note: DAILY_539 5,861 rows fully available — if a future P269B task ever formulates a specific sell_amount-gated hypothesis (e.g., high-sales-day vs low-sales-day hit-rate regime test with pre-registered boundaries), this data is ready without external fetch. However, the EV-only risk is HIGH and such a test is expected to reproduce L101/L102 null.

candidate_matrix.signal_family: jackpot size / rollover count for BIG_LOTTO and POWER_LOTTO
candidate_matrix.source_hint_from_repo: lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db draws.jackpot_amount column schema present (0 rows populated for any lottery); lottery_api/database.py:475-482; P236A classified jackpot sharing as GRAYLIST_PAYOUT_METRIC
candidate_matrix.reason: Jackpot size drives ticket demand (more rolls → higher jackpot → more players). Player count affects prize sharing (EV), not draw randomness (hit-rate). P236A labeled this GRAYLIST_PAYOUT_METRIC. Schema exists but 0 data rows — requires external fetch from TLCAPIWeB. Not a hit-rate signal.

candidate_matrix.signal_family: draw weekday / schedule regime (Mon vs Sat vs holiday calendar segmentation)
candidate_matrix.source_hint_from_repo: draws.date column in lottery_v2.db (all lotteries); P213 hypothesis_categories H_REGIME_SEGMENTATION (design-only, DAILY_539 + POWER_LOTTO scope, P221F gate not passed); P253H draw_history_feature group (days_since_last_draw listed as NOT tested in P219)
candidate_matrix.reason: Calendar regime (weekday/day-of-week/schedule) is not strictly an EXTERNAL signal family — it is derivable from the existing date column in DB. However, P213 H_REGIME_SEGMENTATION explicitly identified this as a valid design direction for DAILY_539 and POWER_LOTTO (design-only, not authorized). P253H vocabulary confirms 'days_since_last_draw' was NOT tested in the P219 M10 MI sweep. DAILY_539 Saturday (806) vs weekday (1015) draws create a natural regime boundary. However, hit-rate plausibility remains LOW for a fair random lottery: weekday identity does not causally affect ball-draw randomness, and L73/L82 closure implies null. OOS feasibility is HIGH (date fully available). This is a WATCHLIST candidate — not a top candidate — because: (1) it is not a truly external data source, (2) hit-rate plausibility is LOW, (3) P221F pre-registration is required before any scan, (4) power per regime is lower than full-history tests that already returned null.

candidate_matrix.signal_family: calendar gap / days_since_last_draw as temporal regime feature
candidate_matrix.source_hint_from_repo: P253H feature vocabulary group 'draw_history_feature': example 'days_since_last_draw (calendar gap)', p219_tested=false; draws.date column in DB; lottery_api/fetcher/missing_issue_detector.py references holiday/cancellation gaps
candidate_matrix.reason: P253H vocabulary explicitly labels 'days_since_last_draw (calendar gap)' as NOT tested in P219 M10 MI sweep. Gap between draws (e.g., post-holiday draws) might reflect changed player behavior or demand patterns, but does not causally affect draw randomness. Hit-rate plausibility is LOW for the same reasons as C05. Data fully available from DB. Structurally overlaps with C05 (weekday regime); testing both simultaneously requires family-wise multiple-testing correction.

candidate_matrix.signal_family: official game-rule / draw machine / ball-set era metadata
candidate_matrix.source_hint_from_repo: P268C: 'P268A confirmed the public API exposes NO machine/ball-set ID field, so era segmentation is not possible from this data source'; P245A B. Bayesian online changepoint detection direction
candidate_matrix.reason: P268C confirmed that TLCAPIWeB exposes no machine/ball-set ID field. This signal family requires either: (a) official press releases / announcements about machine changes, or (b) BOCPD detection of structural breaks as a proxy (P245A recommendation). The BOCPD path is gated on P238B NIST reaching ORANGE/RED (currently YELLOW observation-only). Machine era boundaries are the most theoretically interesting external signal (structural breaks could create actual non-randomness windows), but data is not available in the repo and the API source does not expose this. Oos feasibility is LOW (no source confirmed). Upgrading to TOP_CANDIDATE would require: confirmed source for machine era dates, NIST gate at ORANGE/RED or independent confirmed break evidence, and separate P269B pre-registration.

candidate_matrix.signal_family: official delayed / cancelled / abnormal draw markers
candidate_matrix.source_hint_from_repo: lottery_api/fetcher/missing_issue_detector.py line comment: 'draw numbers are NOT guaranteed to be consecutive integers (holidays, cancellations, year boundaries)'; CURRENT_STATE.md clean gap handling
candidate_matrix.reason: The missing_issue_detector references holiday and cancellation gaps but provides no structured 'abnormal draw' flag in the DB. Cancelled/delayed draws are already handled as gaps in the draw sequence. There is no evidence that abnormal draws have systematically different hit-rate properties. Even if data were available, the causal pathway to hit-rate improvement is unclear. Data availability UNKNOWN (no in-repo structured source).

candidate_matrix.signal_family: official sales data (sell_amount / jackpot) for BIG_LOTTO and POWER_LOTTO
candidate_matrix.source_hint_from_repo: lottery_api/data/lottery_v2.db: draws table has jackpot_amount / sell_amount / total_amount columns for BIG_LOTTO and POWER_LOTTO with 0 rows populated; schema added but not fed
candidate_matrix.reason: Same EV/popularity logic as C03 (DAILY_539 sell_amount). BIG_LOTTO and POWER_LOTTO are jackpot-based games where sales volume affects prize sharing (EV), not draw randomness (hit-rate). Schema exists in DB but 0 rows. Would require: external fetch from TLCAPIWeB, Type D DB write authorization, and a plausible hit-rate hypothesis (none available). Anti-crowd EV already explored in L101/L102 and found marginal advisory-only.

rejected_candidates_summary.key_reason: Fixed-prize lottery, sell_amount is crowd proxy only
Jackpot level affects EV, not P(win)
No structured abnormal draw marker in repo
Same as C03, no data yet

watchlist_candidates.reason_watchlist: In-repo data available (date column); P213 H_REGIME_SEGMENTATION flagged as valid design direction; NOT tested in P219; LOW hit-rate plausibility but untested
watchlist_candidates.required_before_p269b: P221F pre-registration with declared regime boundaries, bonferroni_k, and P221F windows per regime

watchlist_candidates.reason_watchlist: P253H vocabulary confirms NOT tested in P219; in-repo data available; structurally overlaps with C05
watchlist_candidates.required_before_p269b: Same as C05; if tested jointly must declare combined family k

top_candidate_recommendation.reason: From repo-only evidence, no external signal family with HIGH or MEDIUM hit-rate plausibility has been identified that is: (1) not already tested/null, (2) not EV/popularity-only, (3) has available data, and (4) has a plausible causal path to P(win) improvement. The WATCHLIST candidates (C05/C06 calendar regime) are derivable from the existing date column and were flagged in P213 as a legitimate design direction, but they are not truly external signal families, and hit-rate plausibility is LOW for fair random lotteries. The most theoretically interesting external signal (machine/ball-set era, C07) has confirmed unavailable data from the official API. Honest assessment: the repo-only scouting does not surface a strong enough external signal family to justify a full P269B pre-registration effort at this time.
conditions_for_upgrading_to_go: NIST audit reaches ORANGE/RED (P238B gate elevation), enabling BOCPD + machine-era hypothesis (C07)
Official machine/ball-set change dates confirmed from an external source (press releases, official history)
A genuinely new external data source beyond TLCAPIWeB is identified (e.g., third-party draw audit reports)
P245B sequential bias monitor accumulates sufficient evidence (e-value K=100) to reopen a specific game

recommended_p269b_task.title: P269B Calendar Regime Pre-Registration Design (WATCHLIST pathway only)
recommended_p269b_task.condition: Only recommended if user explicitly decides to pursue the WATCHLIST path despite LOW hit-rate plausibility
scope: Type B read-only design doc only
Pre-register weekday/calendar-gap regime boundaries for DAILY_539 and/or POWER_LOTTO
Declare bonferroni_k and P221F windows per regime before any data look
No DB write, no statistical test, no strategy
recommended_p269b_task.worker_model_recommendation: Sonnet 中模型, thinking 中
recommended_p269b_task.caution: If hit-rate plausibility is judged insufficient to justify P269B at all, the system should HOLD and wait for the P245B bias gate or a new external data source.
recommended_p269b_task.alternative_recommendation: HOLD — no P269B at this time. Wait for P245B NIST gate to reach ORANGE/RED, or for explicit user authorization of the WATCHLIST calendar path.
